seoTitle: Configure Scheduled Refresh in Power BI to Automate Data
metaDescription: Learn how to set up Scheduled Refresh in Power BI so your reports update automatically, keeping your data current without manual work.
intro: Manually refreshing your Power BI reports every time your data changes isn't sustainable for ongoing reporting. This guide covers how to configure Scheduled Refresh so your dashboards stay current automatically, keeping your insights accurate without extra effort.

steps.heading: Access Power BI settings
steps.body: Navigate to the settings area for your dataset where Scheduled Refresh options are configured.

steps.heading: Configure refresh frequency
steps.body: Set how often you want your data to refresh, choosing a frequency that matches how often your underlying data actually changes.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for data sources
steps.body: Make sure your connected data sources are set up correctly and accessible, since refresh failures often trace back to source configuration issues.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot refresh issues
steps.body: If a scheduled refresh fails, check the error details and review your data source credentials and connection settings to resolve it.

steps.heading: Apply this for ongoing reporting needs
steps.body: Scheduled Refresh is especially valuable for tracking sales, inventory, or customer behavior, where up-to-date data directly supports better decisions.

faq.question: How often can I schedule a refresh in Power BI?
faq.answer: The available refresh frequency depends on your Power BI license tier, with higher tiers generally allowing more frequent scheduled refreshes.

faq.question: Why does my scheduled refresh keep failing?
faq.answer: Refresh failures are commonly caused by outdated credentials, connectivity issues, or changes to the structure of your underlying data source.

faq.question: Does Scheduled Refresh work with all data sources?
faq.answer: Most common data sources support scheduled refresh, though some may require a gateway connection depending on where the data is hosted.

faq.question: Will Scheduled Refresh use a lot of system resources?
faq.answer: Refresh operations run on Power BI's service rather than your local machine, so it won't burden your own device's resources.
